mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month rise in the unemployment rate above 4.5% signals a tightening labor market that reduces the supply of skilled workers, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in tightening periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05), or the mechanism fails to predict negative returns in > 60% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in housing starts above 2.2 million units signals a strengthening demand for residential real estate, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in strengthening periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in industrial production below 109 indicates a weakening supply chain, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in weakening periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05), or the mechanism fails to predict negative returns in > 60% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in the federal funds rate above 4.5% signals a tightening monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in tightening periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05), or the mechanism fails to predict negative returns in > 60% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in real disposable personal income above 3.5% signals a strengthening demand for consumer goods, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in strengthening periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in initial unemployment claims below 200,000 signals a strengthening labor market that reduces the supply of skilled workers, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in strengthening periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05), or the mechanism fails to predict negative returns in > 60% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in the GDP deflator above 2.5% signals a strengthening supply chain, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in strengthening periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or the mechanism fails to predict positive returns in > 60% of triggered instances.
